PRE00-C.title: PRE00-C. Avoid making assumptions about precedence and associativity
PRE00-C.description: Expressions should not rely on undefined operator precedence.

EXP00-C.title: EXP00-C. Use parentheses for precedence
EXP00-C.description: Do not depend on C's precedence rules.

STR30-C.title: STR30-C. Do not attempt to modify string literals
STR30-C.description: String literals may be stored in read-only memory.

STR31-C.title: STR31-C. Guarantee that storage for strings has sufficient space
STR31-C.description: Ensure that string operations do not overflow buffers.

STR32-C.title: STR32-C. Do not pass a non-null-terminated character sequence to a function that expects a string
STR32-C.description: Passing unterminated sequences can lead to buffer over-reads.

STR38-C.title: STR38-C. Do not confuse functions that accept a wchar_t argument with those that accept a char argument
STR38-C.description: Use the correct character width for string functions.

ARR30-C.title: ARR30-C. Do not form or use out-of-bounds pointers or array subscripts
ARR30-C.description: Array accesses must remain within the bounds of the object.

ARR32-C.title: ARR32-C. Ensure size arguments are appropriate for the array
ARR32-C.description: When passing sizes to memory or string functions, ensure they match the destination.

INT30-C.title: INT30-C. Ensure that unsigned integer operations do not wrap
INT30-C.description: Unsigned integer wraparound can cause unexpected behavior.

INT32-C.title: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in overflow or wraparound
INT32-C.description: Integer overflow can lead to memory corruption or logic errors.

MEM30-C.title: MEM30-C. Do not access freed memory
MEM30-C.description: Use-after-free can lead to crashes or arbitrary code execution.

MEM31-C.title: MEM31-C. Free dynamically allocated memory exactly once
MEM31-C.description: Double-free can corrupt heap metadata and enable exploitation.

MEM34-C.title: MEM34-C. Only free memory allocated dynamically
MEM34-C.description: Freeing non-heap pointers is undefined behavior.

FIO37-C.title: FIO37-C. Ensure that string arguments are null-terminated
FIO37-C.description: File and string I/O require null-terminated buffers.

ERR34-C.title: ERR34-C. Detect errors when converting a string to an integer
ERR34-C.description: Check return values and errno after strtol/strtoul.

MSC37-C.title: MSC37-C. Ensure that control never reaches the end of a non-void function
MSC37-C.description: Non-void functions must return a value on every path.

Dir-1.1.title: Dir-1.1 Any implementation-defined behaviour must be documented and understood
Dir-1.1.description: Avoid reliance on undefined or implementation-defined language behaviour.

Rule-1.3.title: Rule-1.3 There shall be no occurrence of undefined or critical unspecified behaviour

Rule-7.4.title: Rule-7.4 A string literal shall not be assigned to an object unless the object's type is 'pointer to const-qualified char'

Rule-8.14.title: Rule-8.14 The const qualifier shall be used to document read-only objects
Rule-8.14.description: Use const to declare read-only objects.

Rule-14.4.title: Rule-14.4 The condition of an if-statement or iteration statement shall have essentially Boolean type

Rule-17.7.title: Rule-17.7 The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used
Rule-17.7.description: Function return values must not be ignored.

Rule-18.1.title: Rule-18.1 A pointer resulting from arithmetic on a pointer operand shall address an element of the same array or one past the last element
Rule-18.1.description: Pointer arithmetic must stay within array bounds.

Rule-18.2.title: Rule-18.2 Subtraction between pointers shall only address elements of the same array
Rule-18.2.description: Pointer subtraction must be within the same array object.

Rule-18.3.title: Rule-18.3 The relational operators >, >=, < and <= shall not be applied to objects of pointer type except where they point into the same object
Rule-18.3.description: Relational pointer comparisons require same-object pointers.

Rule-18.6.title: Rule-18.6 The address of an object with automatic storage shall not be copied to another object that persists after the first object has ceased to exist
Rule-18.6.description: Avoid returning pointers to local variables.

Rule-20.7.title: Rule-20.7 Expressions resulting from the expansion of macro parameters shall be enclosed in parentheses
Rule-20.7.description: Macro arguments must be parenthesized to avoid precedence issues.

Rule-21.1.title: Rule-21.1 #define and #undef shall not be used on a reserved identifier or reserved macro name
Rule-21.1.description: Do not redefine reserved identifiers or macros.

Rule-21.3.title: Rule-21.3 The memory allocation and deallocation functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used
Rule-21.3.description: Dynamic memory can lead to non-deterministic behaviour.

Rule-21.4.title: Rule-21.4 The standard header file <setjmp.h> shall not be used

Rule-21.6.title: Rule-21.6 The Standard Library input/output functions shall not be used

FIPS-CRYPTO-1.title: Approved cryptographic algorithms only
FIPS-CRYPTO-1.description: Use only FIPS 140-3 approved algorithms (e.g., AES, SHA-2, ECDSA).

FIPS-CRYPTO-2.title: Deterministic random bit generators (DRBG) must be used correctly
FIPS-CRYPTO-2.description: Use a FIPS-approved DRBG and seed it with sufficient entropy.

FIPS-CRYPTO-3.title: Key zeroization and protection
FIPS-CRYPTO-3.description: Cryptographic keys must be zeroized when no longer needed.

FIPS-AUTH-1.title: Identity and authentication controls
FIPS-AUTH-1.description: Authenticate operators before performing cryptographic operations.

FIPS-INTEGRITY-1.title: Software/firmware integrity
FIPS-INTEGRITY-1.description: Maintain integrity of cryptographic module software and firmware.

FIPS-POWER-1.title: Mitigate environmental failure and power analysis
FIPS-POWER-1.description: Protect against side-channel and environmental failure attacks.
